mechanisms[step7-feature-engineering].evidence includes 'pipelines/feature_engineering_pipeline.py:190-197'. The popularity_index assignment spans lines 196-202 (multi-line expression closes at 202). The cited range ends at 197, cutting off the closing parenthesis. The claim is still accurately supported because all five weighted component lines (190-194) and the assignment opening (196) are within range. mechanisms[step1-parallel-dynamodb-scan].claim states 'parallel 20-segment DynamoDB scan'. The step1 code at run_steps.py:87-180 calls self.db_connector.read_table_from_nosql() without any visible segment count in the orchestrator layer. The 20-segment parallelism may live inside DBConnector.read_table_from_nosql() rather than in run_steps.py directly. The claim is consistent with the research brief but cannot be fully confirmed from the cited lines alone. Not blocking — research JSON should be consulted if this is challenged.

recommendations: Operator should update knownIssues[editable-install-absolute-paths].description to name all three -e installs (bkurly-scraper, oliveyoung-scraper, search-trend-analyzer) and add line numbers to the evidence citation.
Optionally extend mechanisms[step7-feature-engineering] evidence range from :190-197 to :190-202 to fully capture the popularity_index assignment.
If the 20-segment DynamoDB scan claim is ever challenged, verify it by inspecting DBConnector.read_table_from_nosql() directly.
